notes: A learned and gifted Christian figure praised by Pope Gregory the Great and Sidonius Apollinaris, noted for charity and refined taste; likely a Roman elite of the late 5th century.

docs.title: Letter 9108
docs.author: Pope Gregory the Great

docs.excerpt: You have placed me deeply in your debt on this account. Moved by that obligation, I cannot refuse your Fraternity's request. Accordingly, in line with what you asked, I have arranged for you to be honored with

docs.title: Letter 5006
docs.author: Venantius Fortunatus
docs.excerpt: … it with wonder — that you have even learned the Frankish language [this is a remarkable detail, as Latin-educated Roman bishops rarely bothered with Germanic vernaculars].
